shortDescription: IRCC Super Visa · Parents/grandparents · 5-year stays · Insurance required

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current Super Visa fee (CAD $100) and minimum medical insurance requirements ($100,000 coverage, 1 year) on canada.ca.

alert.title: Insurance is mandatory
alert.body: Super Visa requires valid Canadian medical insurance for at least 1 year, minimum $100,000 coverage. Without compliant insurance, application will fail.

about.label: Description
about.value: Multi-entry visa allowing parents and grandparents of Canadian citizens/PRs to visit for up to 5 years per entry without renewing status.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Parent/grandparent of Canadian citizen or PR · Child/grandchild meets LICO income threshold · Medical exam · Canadian medical insurance · Ties to home country

about.label: Insurance requirement
about.value: Private medical insurance from Canadian company valid minimum 1 year from entry, $100,000 minimum coverage.

about.label: After approval
about.value: Multi-entry visa; maintain valid insurance on each visit; respect super visa conditions.

eligibility.criterion: Child/grandchild is Canadian citizen or PR

redFlagsBanner: If refused, address medical, insurance, or LICO deficiencies before reapplication.

redFlags.title: Insurance non-compliant
redFlags.description: Coverage below $100,000 or not from Canadian insurer.
redFlags.fix: Purchase IRCC-compliant Super Visa insurance before apply

redFlags.title: LICO income not met
redFlags.description: Sponsor income below minimum for family size.
redFlags.fix: Verify LICO table; combine spouse income if applicable

redFlags.title: Medical inadmissibility
redFlags.fix: Complete exam early; address health issues with counsel

redFlags.title: Weak home ties
redFlags.description: Parent has no property/family remaining in India.
redFlags.fix: Document assets, spouse, and reasons to return

redFlags.title: Incomplete NOA documents
redFlags.description: Missing 3 years Notice of Assessment for sponsor.
redFlags.fix: Collect full 3-year NOA from CRA for sponsor

redFlags.title: Wrong relationship proof
redFlags.fix: Correct birth certificates linking applicant to sponsor

faqs.q: Can we guarantee Super Visa approval?
faqs.a: No. IRCC assesses medical, insurance, LICO, and ties. Never promise approval.
Verify current LICO table on canada.ca for family size including sponsoring household.
Must be Canadian company offering Super Visa compliant coverage—verify with insurer.
Super Visa allows up to 5-year stays per entry; higher LICO and insurance requirements.
Yes; assess combined documentation and sponsor income for family size.
Before application; use IRCC panel physician.
Often 3–8 months—check current IRCC processing times.
No. Visit and family time only—not employment.

compliance: Client service agreement and consent must be on file before submission
Never guarantee visa or immigration approval; use approved-language only
Fee quotes must separate consultancy, government, and third-party costs
proTips: Verify LICO with 3-year NOA before fees
Purchase compliant insurance from known Canadian provider
Link parent to sponsor with clear birth certificates
Document India ties even for elderly parents
postApproval: Remind to maintain valid insurance on each visit

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Added 75 levelled quiz questions (Level 1–3, 25+ each) and expanded counselor content.

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Insurance must be prepaid for 1 year—monthly pay plans often rejected for Super Visa.

quiz.question: Who is the super visa primarily for?
options: Parents and grandparents of Canadian citizens or permanent residents
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: The super visa supports extended visits by eligible parents and grandparents.

quiz.question: What status does a super visa grant?
options: A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: It is not permanent residence or work authorization.

quiz.question: Who usually supports a super visa application in Canada?
options: A child or grandchild who is a citizen or permanent resident
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: The host must have the required relationship and status.

quiz.question: What does LICO mean?
options: A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: The host must meet minimum income requirements based on family size.

quiz.question: Which document is central to super visa eligibility?
options: Private medical insurance meeting IRCC requirements
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Qualifying medical insurance is a core requirement.

quiz.question: How does a super visa differ from a regular visitor visa?
options: It can allow longer stays per entry for eligible parents and grandparents
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: The super visa is designed for extended family visits.

quiz.question: What does multi-entry mean?
options: The holder may seek entry multiple times while the visa is valid
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Each entry is still assessed by border officers.

quiz.question: Who must meet the income requirement?
options: The inviting child or grandchild's household as applicable
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: The Canadian host must show sufficient income.

quiz.question: What proves the host's Canadian status?
options: Canadian passport, citizenship certificate, or PR card
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: The host must prove citizenship or permanent residence.

quiz.question: Which relationship must be documented?
options: Parent or grandparent relationship to the Canadian host
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Civil documents often prove the family link.

quiz.question: Does a super visa allow work in Canada?
options: No, separate work authorization is required
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Super visa holders are visitors.

quiz.question: What is an invitation letter used for?
options: Explaining the visit, support, accommodation, and relationship
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: The invitation organizes the host's support evidence.

quiz.question: Which applicant factor remains important?
options: A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Applicants must satisfy temporary resident requirements.

quiz.question: What is the role of a medical exam?
options: A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Super visa applicants commonly face medical screening.

quiz.question: Does meeting LICO guarantee approval?
options: No, the full application is still assessed
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: LICO is necessary but not the only issue.

quiz.question: What does insurance help cover?
options: Health care costs during the visit
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Private insurance reduces health-cost risk.

quiz.question: Who is not eligible as a principal super visa applicant?
options: A sibling of a Canadian citizen
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: The category is limited to parents and grandparents.

quiz.question: What is the host's financial undertaking?
options: A promise to support the visitor during the stay
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Hosts usually confirm financial support in writing.

quiz.question: Which proof can support host income?
options: Notice of Assessment, pay stubs, and employment letter
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Tax and employment documents commonly prove income.

quiz.question: How is a super visa different from parent sponsorship?
options: Super visa is temporary; sponsorship seeks permanent residence
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: The two processes have different goals.

quiz.question: Can a super visa holder study long-term without a study permit?
options: No, long studies generally require a study permit
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Visitor status has study limits.

quiz.question: What does the applicant's passport support?
options: A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: A valid passport is essential for visa issuance and travel.

quiz.question: Who makes the final entry decision at the border?
options: A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: A visa allows travel to seek entry, not unconditional admission.

quiz.question: Why should insurance dates align with the visit?
options: Coverage must be valid for the required stay period
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Coverage timing and amount must meet current rules.

quiz.question: Why do families choose a super visa?
options: To allow longer visits with parents or grandparents
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: It is useful for extended family visits when eligibility is met.

quiz.question: What belongs in a super visa invitation letter?
options: Host details, relationship, support promise, visit purpose, and accommodation
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: A complete letter connects family, finances, and visit purpose.

quiz.question: How is family size relevant to LICO?
options: A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: LICO is calculated using household and invited-person counts.

quiz.question: Which income document is especially common?
options: A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: A NOA is a common proof of declared Canadian income.

quiz.question: What should be checked in insurance?
options: Coverage amount, duration, provider eligibility, and applicant name
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: The policy must meet IRCC requirements.

quiz.question: Why are birth certificates often used?
options: They prove the parent-child relationship chain
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Civil documents establish the qualifying relationship.

quiz.question: How should name differences be handled?
options: Provide marriage, name-change, or explanation documents
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Name differences should be reconciled with evidence.

quiz.question: Which evidence can support home-country ties?
options: Property, family, employment, pension, or community obligations
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Tie evidence supports temporary intent.

quiz.question: Why present travel history accurately?
options: It helps officers assess past visa compliance
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Omissions can damage credibility.

quiz.question: What support can a host document besides income?
options: A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Hosts can explain where the visitor will stay and how costs will be covered.

quiz.question: Why avoid non-refundable tickets too early?
options: Visa approval and entry are not guaranteed
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Travel plans should account for processing uncertainty.

quiz.question: Which applicant documents support identity and civil status?
options: Passport and marriage certificate if applicable
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Identity and family documents establish background.

quiz.question: How can a retired parent show finances?
options: Pension, savings, property, and family support evidence
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Retired applicants can document economic ties and stability.

quiz.question: What if the host recently changed jobs?
options: Provide current employment proof and income history
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Current and historical evidence can show LICO is still met.

quiz.question: What makes a super visa application stronger?
options: Clear purpose of visit with realistic duration and support
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: A credible temporary plan helps address officer concerns.

quiz.question: What should happen after a medical request?
options: Complete the exam with an approved panel physician by the deadline
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: IRCC medicals must be completed with panel physicians.

quiz.question: Why is host marital status relevant?
options: Spouse and dependants may affect family size
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Household composition affects the income threshold.

quiz.question: What if both parents apply together?
options: Family size and support evidence must account for both
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Inviting multiple people raises the LICO calculation.

quiz.question: What can prove the host resides in Canada?
options: Lease, utility bill, or employment letter
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Residence evidence supports the host's ability to receive visitors.

quiz.question: Why must forms list family accurately?
options: Incorrect family information can create credibility issues
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Truthful family disclosure is required.

quiz.question: What should a self-employed host provide?
options: Tax filings, business income records, and financial documents
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Self-employed income should be clearly documented.

quiz.question: How can a grandparent relationship be proven?
options: Birth certificates linking grandparent to parent to grandchild
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: A document chain may be needed.

quiz.question: Why review insurance cancellation terms?
options: Families should understand refunds and policy compliance
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: The policy must meet IRCC rules and practical needs.

quiz.question: Why upload the applicant's financial evidence?
options: It supports ties, travel ability, and temporary intent
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Applicant finances complement host support.

quiz.question: How are other relatives handled?
options: They may need separate visitor options
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: The super visa is only for parents and grandparents.

quiz.question: What is good practice before submission?
options: Cross-check names, dates, insurance, LICO, relationship proof, and forms
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: A consistency review reduces avoidable problems.

quiz.question: Why can a prior overstay affect a super visa?
options: It raises concerns about compliance with temporary status
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Past compliance is relevant to future temporary resident decisions.

quiz.question: What is risky about an indefinite visit plan?
options: A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Super visa holders remain temporary residents.

quiz.question: Why must host income evidence be current?
options: Officers must be satisfied LICO is met at assessment
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Current reliable income evidence is central.

quiz.question: What is complex for hosts with variable income?
options: Proving stable income above LICO despite fluctuations
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Commission or self-employment income may need stronger documents.

quiz.question: Why disclose previous refusals?
options: Undisclosed refusals can lead to misrepresentation findings
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Immigration history must be complete and truthful.

quiz.question: What if insurance is from a non-qualifying provider?
options: The application may fail the insurance requirement
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Provider and coverage rules must be satisfied.

quiz.question: Why respect border entry conditions after approval?
options: Stay length and conditions are determined at entry and by law
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Visa approval does not remove visitor conditions.

quiz.question: What if a super visa holder works without authorization?
options: It can jeopardize status and future applications
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Visitor status does not authorize work.

quiz.question: Why is medical inadmissibility still relevant?
options: Insurance does not eliminate health admissibility assessment
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: IRCC may still assess medical admissibility.

quiz.question: How should weak home ties be addressed?
options: Provide concrete evidence of obligations, assets, family, and return reasons
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Specific evidence is stronger than general promises.

quiz.question: What is risky about relying only on host documents?
options: The applicant's own temporary intent may remain unproven
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Both host eligibility and applicant circumstances matter.

quiz.question: Why can family-size errors cause refusal?
options: They may make LICO appear met when it is not
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Accurate household counts are essential.

quiz.question: What if host income is below LICO?
options: Do not claim eligibility until qualifying income exists
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: False or insufficient income evidence creates refusal risk.

quiz.question: Why does the officer examine visit purpose?
options: To decide whether the plan is credible and temporary
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Purpose of visit is a core temporary resident factor.

quiz.question: Which issue may need an explanation letter?
options: Previous refusal, name discrepancy, or unusually long intended stay
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Explanation letters clarify facts that may concern an officer.

quiz.question: What is the risk of altered bank statements?
options: A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Fraudulent documents are serious immigration violations.

quiz.question: Why can frequent long stays be scrutinized?
options: They may suggest living in Canada rather than visiting
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: The person must remain a temporary resident visitor.

quiz.question: How should a previous super visa refusal be addressed?
options: Identify refusal reasons and submit new evidence addressing them
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: A reapplication should respond directly to earlier concerns.

quiz.question: Why avoid overstating support capacity?
options: A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Support promises should match documented income and obligations.

quiz.question: What if a parent has limited property or employment abroad?
options: The file may need stronger family, financial, and social tie evidence
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Weak economic ties can be balanced with other credible return reasons.

quiz.question: What is procedural fairness in a super visa case?
options: A chance to respond to serious concerns such as misrepresentation
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: A timely evidence-based response is important.

quiz.question: Why can incomplete family disclosure affect future sponsorship?
options: It can create credibility and eligibility problems later
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Family information should be complete and consistent.

quiz.question: What before extending a stay beyond authorized time?
options: Apply to extend visitor status before expiry
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Visitors must maintain status if staying longer.

quiz.question: Why is refusal analysis useful before reapplying?
options: It targets actual weaknesses instead of random documents
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Understanding refusal grounds supports a better application.

quiz.question: What is the best strategy for a complex super visa file?
options: Prove host eligibility, applicant ties, admissibility, and temporary purpose
A work permit for Canadian employers
quiz.explanation: Complex cases need evidence for every likely officer concern.

donts: Do not apply if LICO not met
Do not quote outdated LICO amounts
mistakes: LICO calculated wrong for family size

sampleDocs.title: Sample passport bio page (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Show clients which pages to scan — MRZ visible, no glare, full spread.

sampleDocs.title: Sample Super Visa insurance policy (mock)
sampleDocs.description: 1-year minimum coverage from Canadian insurer — explain to parents.

sampleDocs.title: Sample invitation letter from child (mock)
sampleDocs.description: Canadian child PR/citizen invitation with household details.

sampleDocs.title: Sample child income proof (mock)
sampleDocs.description: NOA or employment letter showing LICO threshold met.

sampleDocs.title: Sample bank statement (mock)
sampleDocs.description: 6-month statement with name, account number, and closing balance — explain seasoning.
